reason: The snippet exists exactly at RIGHT line 4 in the diff; mutable default argument is a genuine Python anti-pattern flagged by all major linters and worth human attention.
comment: Mutable default argument `defaults={}` is created once at function-definition time and shared across all calls that omit the argument. While `defaults` is never mutated in this function, it is a well-known Python anti-pattern: any future edit that modifies `defaults` inside the function would cause silent cross-call state leakage. The fix is `defaults=None` with `if defaults is None: defaults = {}` at the top of the body.
